top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Istria Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
65 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Istria Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 65 miles.
71ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Istria Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Istria Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Istria Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Base fitness for Istria Gran Fondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Low-cadence force work for 4,600ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.heat: April in Umag, Istria, Croatia can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Istria Gran Fondo. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Istria Gran Fondo’s 65 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Istria Gran Fondo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Durability, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 65 miles of mixed terrain in Umag, Istria, Croatia.
